note: ranno nago, sangamagato hatthikayam = royal war-elephant (Akkhama-sutta)
uddana: nagamigasala (Migasala-sutta) + nagito (elder Nagita)

uddana catchwords (nagasirivhayano = 'named Nagasiri', plus bare naga/nago); underlying jatakas not in window

claim: a naga has a spread hood (phana) and coils (bhoga) and caught a falling prince with its coils
note: 'Nago jataphano tattha, thamava... paccaggahi mam bhogehi' = serpent form

Bhisa Jataka identifications: 'Palileyyo tada nago' = the Parileyya elephant

claim: the naga-clan (with its maidens and dwellings) lives under the supannas' predatory enmity; the king secures supanna non-aggression toward the nagas
note: Bhuridatta/Sankhapala: nagakula, nagakanna, supanna enmity, nagassa nivesanani; also realm_habitat, bodhisatta_as_naga
visible window = Chaddanta (hunter cuts dantani gajuttamassa, elephant-herd mourns); mixed (tokens also Pandara-naga-king = serpent)

claim: a naga, by its power, took the form of a ship to carry a faithful layman safely across the sea
note: Silanisamsa Jataka: 'Nago navaya vannena, saddham vahatupasakam' = shapeshift into a vessel
uddana catchwords (nagasirivhayano 'named Nagasiri', nagametam, nago); underlying jatakas not in window
Jambuka Jataka: jackal attacks the gaja/elephant (gajamasajja) and 'seti nagena hato'
Latukika Jataka: the elephant (naga/nagaraja) that trampled the quail's chicks is killed; prov hint 'serpent' wrong; polysemy
river naga: 'Gangaya sotasmi gahitanavam, luddena nagena' seized a boat, overcome; narrative, aquatic naga
Kosambiya Jataka: 'eko care matanga-rann-eva nago' = solitary forest tusker-elephant simile

claim: a mighty naga-king of divine power keeps austerities/uposatha longing for human rebirth, holding the human world superior (for the path)
note: 'Manussayonim abhipatthayano... tapo karomi'; also uposatha, takes_human_form, bodhisatta_as_naga; naganasa (elephant-trunk) simile also in tokens
'Satthi nagasahassani... matanga, ankusa, arulha gamaniyehi' = 60,000 royal elephants with goad-bearing drivers
'naganasuru' = elephant-trunk(-like) thighs, beauty epithet of Pabhavati
forest tree-list: punnagabakula..., asoka-nagarukkha (the naga/ironwood tree)-candana...; mixed (compound also nagakula serpent, karenu/susunaga elephant)

claim: Bhuridatta Jataka: the Bodhisatta as a naga-king, with naga-maidens (Irandhati), the naga-clan and naga-realm, and naga marriage
note: Mahanipata; nagavara, nagakanna, nagohamasmi, Varuno nago; also realm_habitat, uposatha, takes_human_form
